name: Revolcadores Trail (PR-MU 105)
name_original: El Sendero de los Obispos (PR-MU 105) — Tour desde Inazares
description: Hike the moderate 7.4-mile Revolcadores Trail (PR-MU 105) in Murcia's highest massif, gaining 2248 feet for expansive views.
region_intro: Hiking around Moratalla offers diverse natural landscapes in the northwest of the Region of Murcia, Spain. The area is characterized by mountainous terrain, including the Sierra de Moratalla, with extensive woodlands of pines, holm oaks, and junipers. Crystal-clear rivers like the Alhárabe and Benamor carve through the landscape, forming natural pools such as the Pozas de Somogil and geological features like the Bolvonegro Strait. This varied terrain provides numerous opportunities for exploring Moratalla hiking landmarks and natural features. Best hiking trails around Moratalla The most popular hiking route is El Rio Alharabe — Circular until the Banos del Somogil, a 4.7 miles (7.6 km) moderate trail that takes 2 hours 9 minutes to complete. This route follows the Alhárabe River, leading to refreshing natural pools. Another top favourite among local hikers is La Sierra Moratalla — Circular to the Hondares stream waterfall, a difficult 11.4 miles (18.4 km) path. This trail explores the mountainous Sierra Moratalla, featuring significant elevation changes and a waterfall. Local hikers also love the The Bishop's Trail (PR-MU 105) — Tour from Inazares, a 7.4 miles (11.9 km) trail leading through mountainous terrain near Inazares, often completed in about 4 hours 9 minutes. Hiking around Moratalla is defined by mountainous terrain, extensive woodlands, and crystal-clear rivers. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate paths to more challenging ascents. The routes in Moratalla are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.8 stars from more than 100 reviews. More than 600 hikers have used komoot to explore Moratalla's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: What kind of natural features can I expect to see on hikes around Moratalla?
A: Moratalla's hiking trails showcase a rich variety of natural features. You'll find extensive woodlands of pines, holm oaks, and junipers, especially in the Sierra de Moratalla. Crystal-clear rivers like the Alhárabe and Benamor carve through the landscape, forming refreshing natural pools such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4852802" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Somogil Baths&lt;/a&gt; and dramatic geological formations like the Bolvonegro Strait. The region also boasts mountainous terrain, including the impressive Revolcadores Peak.

Q: Are there any hikes that feature waterfalls or natural pools?
A: Yes, Moratalla is known for its beautiful water features. The Alhárabe River forms the refreshing Pozas de Somogil, a series of natural pools perfect for a dip. You can explore this area on the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e897943352/el-rio-alharabe-circular-until-the-banos-del-somogil" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;El Rio Alharabe — Circular until the Banos del Somogil&lt;/a&gt; trail. Additionally, the Hondares Stream features a waterfall, also known as Poza de las Tortugas, which can be reached via the more challenging &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e900452463/la-sierra-moratalla-circular-to-the-hondares-stream-waterfall" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Sierra Moratalla — Circular to the Hondares stream waterfall&lt;/a&gt; route.

Q: Are there any family-friendly hiking options in Moratalla?
A: Yes, Moratalla offers several routes suitable for families. Shorter, less strenuous paths along the rivers, such as sections of the Alhárabe River, are often ideal. The 'Hiking in Bolvonegro' route is specifically mentioned as a shorter option, great for families. Look for trails with moderate difficulty and shorter distances for a pleasant family outing.

Q: What are the best times of year to go hiking in Moratalla?
A: The best times to hike in Moratalla are typically spring and autumn. During these seasons, the weather is milder and more pleasant for outdoor activities, avoiding the intense heat of summer and the colder, potentially wetter conditions of winter. Spring brings blooming wildflowers, while autumn offers beautiful foliage.

Q: Are there any circular hiking routes available?
A: Yes, Moratalla has several excellent circular routes. Popular options include &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e897943352/el-rio-alharabe-circular-until-the-banos-del-somogil" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;El Rio Alharabe — Circular until the Banos del Somogil&lt;/a&gt;, which follows the river, and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38220903" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Moratalla Pools loop from El Molino&lt;/a&gt;. For a more challenging circular experience, consider &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e900452463/la-sierra-moratalla-circular-to-the-hondares-stream-waterfall" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Sierra Moratalla — Circular to the Hondares stream waterfall&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What historical or cultural landmarks can I visit while hiking in Moratalla?
A: Moratalla's trails often combine natural beauty with historical interest. The town itself features the imposing 9th-century Castle of Moratalla and the 16th-century Hermitage of La Rogativa, both offering panoramic views. You can also find the Roman bridge over the Alhárabe River, adding historical context to river walks. The region is also rich in prehistoric rock art, with sites like Cueva de Zaen and the Caña shelters declared World Heritage.

Q: How many hiking trails are there in Moratalla, and what are their difficulty levels?
A: There are over 35 hiking trails around Moratalla. These routes cater to various fitness levels, with 9 easy trails, 20 moderate trails, and 7 difficult trails, ensuring options for everyone from casual walkers to experienced hikers seeking a challenge.

Q: What do other hikers enjoy most about the trails in Moratalla?
A: The trails in Moratalla are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.8 stars from over 100 reviews. Hikers frequently praise the diverse natural landscapes, including the extensive woodlands and crystal-clear rivers, as well as the variety of routes suitable for different ability levels. The opportunity to explore both natural beauty and historical sites is also a common highlight.

Q: Are there any specific trails that lead to viewpoints or offer panoramic views?
A: Yes, many trails in Moratalla offer stunning views, particularly those in the mountainous areas. Climbing &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/5292219" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Revolcadores Peak&lt;/a&gt;, the highest point in the Murcia Region, provides breathtaking panoramic vistas. The Sierra de Moratalla itself, explored by routes like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e896197266/the-bishop-s-trail-pr-mu-105-tour-from-inazares" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;The Bishop's Trail (PR-MU 105)&lt;/a&gt;, also features significant elevation changes that lead to impressive viewpoints.

Q: Is it possible to combine hiking with visiting other natural attractions nearby?
A: Absolutely. Moratalla is surrounded by other natural attractions that can complement your hiking experience. You can explore nearby gorges like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5696563" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cañón de los Almacenes&lt;/a&gt; or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1644830" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Walkway of the Río Mundo Canyon&lt;/a&gt;. The region also features several lakes, including &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2745212" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Charco de Las Canales&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5588795" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Charco Pataco&lt;/a&gt;, which offer additional opportunities for nature appreciation.

Q: Are there any areas with unique geological formations or ancient history?
A: Moratalla is rich in both geological and archaeological wonders. The Bolvonegro Strait, carved by the Alhárabe River, showcases beautiful sedimentary rock formations. Along the rivers, you can observe ancient marine shells and strata, offering glimpses into the area's distant past. Furthermore, the region is home to the largest collection of Mediterranean rock art in Murcia, with sites like El Calar de la Santa and the Caña shelters, which are World Heritage sites.
tips: The Pico de los Obispos is the highest in the Province of Murcia. With an altitude of 2014 meters above sea level, you will have amazing views in all directions. You can get here by the Revolcadores Path starting from Inazares. Remember to wear suitable footwear and plenty of water, especially if you go in summer.

faqs.question: Where does the Revolcadores Trail (PR-MU 105) start and where can I park?
faqs.answer: The Komoot tour for the Revolcadores Trail (PR-MU 105) starts from Inazares. While specific parking details for this exact trailhead aren't provided, the trail itself is described as easy to follow. For the broader Revolcadores area, hikers often look for parking near trailheads, so it's advisable to check local maps or recent hiker reports for the most convenient parking spots in Inazares or nearby access points.

faqs.question: How challenging is the Revolcadores Trail (PR-MU 105)?
faqs.answer: This specific section of the Revolcadores Trail (PR-MU 105) is rated as moderate. It's a 3 km multi-use dirt/gravel road with a blue climb difficulty of 43 meters, making it generally easy to follow. However, the wider Revolcadores massif offers more strenuous routes, so be aware that other trails in the area can be more demanding.

faqs.question: What kind of terrain will I encounter on the trail?
faqs.answer: The PR-MU 105 section primarily features a dirt/gravel road, making it relatively straightforward to navigate. In the broader Revolcadores massif, you might encounter more varied terrain, including small ravines (barranquillos), some climbs (trepadas), dense scrubland, and pine forests. The area is also known for its limestone geology, which creates interesting formations like sinkholes.

faqs.question: What are the best viewpoints along the route?
faqs.answer: The Revolcadores massif, being the highest area in the Murcia region, offers expansive vistas. Along the trail, you can expect beautiful views of surrounding peaks like La Sagra and La Guillimona. The area is praised for its "wonderful landscape" and impressive natural beauty, providing many opportunities for scenic overlooks.

faqs.question: What wildlife might I see on the Revolcadores Trail (PR-MU 105)?
faqs.answer: The Revolcadores massif is a significant area for biodiversity. As a Reserve Zone within the Natura 2000 network, it's a place where you might observe diverse wildlife. Keep an eye out for birds of prey such as eagles and griffon vultures, as well as mammals like mountain goats and wild boar.

faqs.question: When is the best time of year to hike this trail?
faqs.answer: While not explicitly stated, the Revolcadores massif is known for its high elevation. Generally, spring and autumn offer the most pleasant hiking conditions with milder temperatures. Summer can be very hot, and winter might bring snow to the higher elevations, making conditions more challenging. Always check the weather forecast before you go.

faqs.question: Are there any specific landmarks or natural features to look out for?
faqs.answer: Yes, the trail is located within the Revolcadores massif, which is home to the highest peaks in the Murcia region. A notable point of interest is Pico de los Obispos, one of the highest points. The area also features unique geological formations like sinkholes and cavities due to limestone erosion.

faqs.question: Is the Revolcadores Trail (PR-MU 105) suitable for dogs?
faqs.answer: The trail itself is a multi-use dirt/gravel road, which generally makes it accessible for dogs. However, as it's located within a protected area (ZONA DE RESERVA), it's crucial to check local regulations regarding dogs, especially leash rules, to ensure the protection of local wildlife and habitats. Always practice responsible pet ownership.

faqs.question: Do I need a permit or is there an entrance fee for the Revolcadores massif?
faqs.answer: The summit of Revolcadores is designated as a "ZONA DE RESERVA" (Reserve Zone) within the Special Conservation Zone of Revolcadores, integrated into the Natura 2000 network. While there isn't typically an entrance fee for hiking in natural parks in Spain, specific activities or access to certain sensitive areas might require permits. It's always best to check with local park authorities for the most current regulations before your visit.

faqs.question: Is the trail suitable for families with children?
faqs.answer: Given its moderate difficulty rating, 3 km length, and easy-to-follow dirt/gravel road terrain, the PR-MU 105 section of the Revolcadores Trail can be suitable for families with children who are accustomed to hiking. The relatively short distance and clear path make it manageable, but always consider your children's fitness levels and bring plenty of water and snacks.

faqs.question: What should I bring for a hike on this trail?
faqs.answer: For this moderate trail, essential items include sturdy hiking shoes, sufficient water, snacks, sun protection (hat, sunscreen), and appropriate clothing layers for changing mountain weather. Given the potential for varied terrain in the broader massif, a basic first-aid kit and a map or GPS device are also recommended. If hiking with dogs, remember their water and waste bags.
